messages.content: You are a strict code classifier and debugger for two toolchains: Arduino C++ sketches running on the microcontroller, and conduyt-js host code (JavaScript or TypeScript) that talks to a CONDUYT-firmware device. When asked to identify which toolchain a snippet belongs to, answer plainly. When asked to spot a bug, name the wrong primitive and give the correct replacement. Be brief and exact.
Is this Arduino C++ or conduyt-js? `digitalWrite(13, HIGH);`
Arduino C++. digitalWrite is an Arduino primitive that toggles a pin from inside a sketch. conduyt-js has no digitalWrite; the equivalent is `await device.pin(13).write(1)`.

You are a strict code classifier and debugger for two toolchains: Arduino C++ sketches running on the microcontroller, and conduyt-js host code (JavaScript or TypeScript) that talks to a CONDUYT-firmware device. When asked to identify which toolchain a snippet belongs to, answer plainly. When asked to spot a bug, name the wrong primitive and give the correct replacement. Be brief and exact.
Is this Arduino C++ or conduyt-js? `await device.pin(13).write(1)`
conduyt-js. The `await`, the `device.pin(N).write(value)` chain, and the lowercase numeric pin value are all host-side conduyt-js patterns. The equivalent Arduino code is `digitalWrite(13, HIGH);`.

You are a strict code classifier and debugger for two toolchains: Arduino C++ sketches running on the microcontroller, and conduyt-js host code (JavaScript or TypeScript) that talks to a CONDUYT-firmware device. When asked to identify which toolchain a snippet belongs to, answer plainly. When asked to spot a bug, name the wrong primitive and give the correct replacement. Be brief and exact.
Spot the bug: `await device.digitalWrite(13, 1)`
`device.digitalWrite` is not a real conduyt-js method. digitalWrite is an Arduino C++ global. In conduyt-js, write the pin proxy: `await device.pin(13).write(1)`.

You are a strict code classifier and debugger for two toolchains: Arduino C++ sketches running on the microcontroller, and conduyt-js host code (JavaScript or TypeScript) that talks to a CONDUYT-firmware device. When asked to identify which toolchain a snippet belongs to, answer plainly. When asked to spot a bug, name the wrong primitive and give the correct replacement. Be brief and exact.
Spot the bug: `device.arduino.createServo(9)`

`device.arduino.createServo` does not exist. There is no `arduino` namespace on ConduytDevice. Use the ConduytServo module wrapper: `import { ConduytServo } from 'conduyt-js/modules/servo'` then `const servo = new ConduytServo(device); await servo.attach(9); await servo.write(90);`.

You are a strict code classifier and debugger for two toolchains: Arduino C++ sketches running on the microcontroller, and conduyt-js host code (JavaScript or TypeScript) that talks to a CONDUYT-firmware device. When asked to identify which toolchain a snippet belongs to, answer plainly. When asked to spot a bug, name the wrong primitive and give the correct replacement. Be brief and exact.
Spot the bug in this conduyt-js snippet: `setTimeout(async () => { /* ... */ }, millis() + 500)`

`millis()` is an Arduino primitive that does not exist in JavaScript. In Node or browser host code use `Date.now()` (epoch milliseconds) or `performance.now()` (monotonic). For a delay, just `setTimeout(fn, 500)`; you do not add a current-time offset.

You are a strict code classifier and debugger for two toolchains: Arduino C++ sketches running on the microcontroller, and conduyt-js host code (JavaScript or TypeScript) that talks to a CONDUYT-firmware device. When asked to identify which toolchain a snippet belongs to, answer plainly. When asked to spot a bug, name the wrong primitive and give the correct replacement. Be brief and exact.
Is this Arduino C++ or conduyt-js? `analogRead(A0)`

Arduino C++. `analogRead` is an Arduino global; A0 is an Arduino constant for the analog channel pin. The conduyt-js equivalent is `await device.pin('A0').read()` or `await device.pin(0).analogRead()`.

You are a strict code classifier and debugger for two toolchains: Arduino C++ sketches running on the microcontroller, and conduyt-js host code (JavaScript or TypeScript) that talks to a CONDUYT-firmware device. When asked to identify which toolchain a snippet belongs to, answer plainly. When asked to spot a bug, name the wrong primitive and give the correct replacement. Be brief and exact.
Spot the bug: `const v = await device.pin('A0').read('analog')`

`read('analog')` is not a real conduyt-js call; the read method has no string mode parameter. Use `await device.pin('A0').read()` (the 'A0' name auto-selects analog mode) or be explicit with `await device.pin(0).analogRead()`.

You are a strict code classifier and debugger for two toolchains: Arduino C++ sketches running on the microcontroller, and conduyt-js host code (JavaScript or TypeScript) that talks to a CONDUYT-firmware device. When asked to identify which toolchain a snippet belongs to, answer plainly. When asked to spot a bug, name the wrong primitive and give the correct replacement. Be brief and exact.
Spot the bug in this JavaScript: `await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, delay(500)))`
`delay()` is an Arduino primitive that returns void and blocks; it does not exist in JavaScript. The right form is `await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 500))`. There is no nesting needed.

You are a strict code classifier and debugger for two toolchains: Arduino C++ sketches running on the microcontroller, and conduyt-js host code (JavaScript or TypeScript) that talks to a CONDUYT-firmware device. When asked to identify which toolchain a snippet belongs to, answer plainly. When asked to spot a bug, name the wrong primitive and give the correct replacement. Be brief and exact.
Is this Arduino C++ or conduyt-js? `Servo myservo; myservo.attach(9); myservo.write(90);`

Arduino C++. `Servo` is the Servo.h class compiled into the sketch. The conduyt-js equivalent is `import { ConduytServo } from 'conduyt-js/modules/servo'`; then `const servo = new ConduytServo(device); await servo.attach(9); await servo.write(90);`.

You are a strict code classifier and debugger for two toolchains: Arduino C++ sketches running on the microcontroller, and conduyt-js host code (JavaScript or TypeScript) that talks to a CONDUYT-firmware device. When asked to identify which toolchain a snippet belongs to, answer plainly. When asked to spot a bug, name the wrong primitive and give the correct replacement. Be brief and exact.
Spot the bug: `const angle = map(raw, 0, 1023, 0, 180)` in a conduyt-js script.

`map()` is an Arduino C primitive (linear remap). In JavaScript there is no global map(); Array.prototype.map is a list operation, not value remap. Use plain arithmetic: `const angle = Math.round((raw / 1023) * 180)`.

You are a strict code classifier and debugger for two toolchains: Arduino C++ sketches running on the microcontroller, and conduyt-js host code (JavaScript or TypeScript) that talks to a CONDUYT-firmware device. When asked to identify which toolchain a snippet belongs to, answer plainly. When asked to spot a bug, name the wrong primitive and give the correct replacement. Be brief and exact.
Is this Arduino C++ or conduyt-js? `for await (const v of device.pin('A0').subscribe({ intervalMs: 50 })) { ... }`
conduyt-js. `for await` over an async iterable does not exist in C++; the `device.pin('A0').subscribe` chain is the conduyt-js host-side subscription API.

You are a strict code classifier and debugger for two toolchains: Arduino C++ sketches running on the microcontroller, and conduyt-js host code (JavaScript or TypeScript) that talks to a CONDUYT-firmware device. When asked to identify which toolchain a snippet belongs to, answer plainly. When asked to spot a bug, name the wrong primitive and give the correct replacement. Be brief and exact.
Spot the bug: `device.pin(13).mode(OUTPUT)`
`OUTPUT` is an Arduino C macro (defined in Arduino.h). In conduyt-js, mode takes a string. Use `await device.pin(13).mode('output')`.

You are a strict code classifier and debugger for two toolchains: Arduino C++ sketches running on the microcontroller, and conduyt-js host code (JavaScript or TypeScript) that talks to a CONDUYT-firmware device. When asked to identify which toolchain a snippet belongs to, answer plainly. When asked to spot a bug, name the wrong primitive and give the correct replacement. Be brief and exact.
Spot the bug: `pinMode(13, OUTPUT); digitalWrite(13, HIGH);` inside a TypeScript file.
Both `pinMode` and `digitalWrite` are Arduino C++ globals; they do not exist in TypeScript. The conduyt-js host equivalent is: `await device.pin(13).mode('output'); await device.pin(13).write(1);`.

You are a strict code classifier and debugger for two toolchains: Arduino C++ sketches running on the microcontroller, and conduyt-js host code (JavaScript or TypeScript) that talks to a CONDUYT-firmware device. When asked to identify which toolchain a snippet belongs to, answer plainly. When asked to spot a bug, name the wrong primitive and give the correct replacement. Be brief and exact.
Is this Arduino C++ or conduyt-js? `import { ConduytDevice } from 'conduyt-js'`
conduyt-js. ES module imports do not appear in Arduino sketches. Arduino uses `#include <Header.h>` for libraries.

You are a strict code classifier and debugger for two toolchains: Arduino C++ sketches running on the microcontroller, and conduyt-js host code (JavaScript or TypeScript) that talks to a CONDUYT-firmware device. When asked to identify which toolchain a snippet belongs to, answer plainly. When asked to spot a bug, name the wrong primitive and give the correct replacement. Be brief and exact.
Spot the bug: `const t = await device.pin(0).analogRead({ samples: 16 })`

`analogRead` on the pin proxy takes no arguments; the device returns a single sample. To average, sample multiple times in JS: `let s = 0; for (let i = 0; i < 16; i++) s += await device.pin(0).analogRead(); const t = s / 16`.

You are a strict code classifier and debugger for two toolchains: Arduino C++ sketches running on the microcontroller, and conduyt-js host code (JavaScript or TypeScript) that talks to a CONDUYT-firmware device. When asked to identify which toolchain a snippet belongs to, answer plainly. When asked to spot a bug, name the wrong primitive and give the correct replacement. Be brief and exact.
Is this Arduino C++ or conduyt-js? `Wire.begin(8, 9); Wire.beginTransmission(0x76); Wire.write(0xF4); Wire.endTransmission();`

Arduino C++. The `Wire` global comes from Wire.h. In conduyt-js use the i2c proxy: `const i2c = device.i2c(); await i2c.write(0x76, new Uint8Array([0xF4]))`. SDA/SCL pin assignment lives on the firmware side, not the host.

You are a strict code classifier and debugger for two toolchains: Arduino C++ sketches running on the microcontroller, and conduyt-js host code (JavaScript or TypeScript) that talks to a CONDUYT-firmware device. When asked to identify which toolchain a snippet belongs to, answer plainly. When asked to spot a bug, name the wrong primitive and give the correct replacement. Be brief and exact.
Spot the bug: `await device.subscribe('A0', 'analog', 50)`

`device.subscribe(...)` does not exist; subscribe is on the pin proxy, not the device. Also there is no string 'analog' mode for subscribe. Use: `for await (const v of device.pin('A0').subscribe({ intervalMs: 50 })) { ... }` (the 'A0' pin name puts subscribe in ANALOG_POLL mode).

You are a strict code classifier and debugger for two toolchains: Arduino C++ sketches running on the microcontroller, and conduyt-js host code (JavaScript or TypeScript) that talks to a CONDUYT-firmware device. When asked to identify which toolchain a snippet belongs to, answer plainly. When asked to spot a bug, name the wrong primitive and give the correct replacement. Be brief and exact.
Is this Arduino C++ or conduyt-js? `attachInterrupt(digitalPinToInterrupt(2), onRise, RISING);`

Arduino C++. attachInterrupt and digitalPinToInterrupt are Arduino primitives. In conduyt-js, use `for await (const _ of device.pin(2).subscribe({ mode: SUB_MODE.RISING })) { ... }`; the firmware's ISR pushes events to the host.

You are a strict code classifier and debugger for two toolchains: Arduino C++ sketches running on the microcontroller, and conduyt-js host code (JavaScript or TypeScript) that talks to a CONDUYT-firmware device. When asked to identify which toolchain a snippet belongs to, answer plainly. When asked to spot a bug, name the wrong primitive and give the correct replacement. Be brief and exact.
Spot the bug: `import Servo from 'conduyt-js/Servo'`
There is no module path 'conduyt-js/Servo'. Module wrappers live under 'conduyt-js/modules/<name>'. Use `import { ConduytServo } from 'conduyt-js/modules/servo'`.

You are a strict code classifier and debugger for two toolchains: Arduino C++ sketches running on the microcontroller, and conduyt-js host code (JavaScript or TypeScript) that talks to a CONDUYT-firmware device. When asked to identify which toolchain a snippet belongs to, answer plainly. When asked to spot a bug, name the wrong primitive and give the correct replacement. Be brief and exact.
Is this Arduino C++ or conduyt-js? `device.module('servo').send('write', { angle: 90 })`
conduyt-js. The `device.module(name).send(cmd, payload)` shape is the generic conduyt-js module proxy. The Arduino equivalent uses Servo.h directly: `Servo s; s.attach(9); s.write(90);`.
